params.prompt:
[Sources: web]

Use browser automation to gather and synthesise a practical proposal: 1) Open Hacker News front page and capture top 5 themes. 2) Open GitHub trending and capture top 5 repositories with language and one-line summary. 3) Produce a phased 1-week execution plan for a solo developer to build one idea inspired by those trends. 4) Include risks, mitigations, and a compact budget/time table. Keep the final answer concise, structured, and cite gathered web evidence inline.

result.final_answer: <answer>- ### Hacker News Top 5 Themes 1. **AI/LLMs/Software Engineering:** Discussions around AI, large language models (LLMs), AI agents, code generation, and software verification [web:1]. 2. **Hardware/OS Advancements:** Focus on new processors (M5 Pro/Max, Intel 18A), mobile OS developments (GrapheneOS), and hardware repairability [web:1]. 3. **Privacy/Security Concerns:** Topics include end-to-end encryption debates and identity/age verification challenges [web:1]. 4. **Graphics/Game Development:** Resources for graphics programming and innovative game engines/languages [web:1]. 5. **Scientific/Research Computing:** Articles on complex computational concepts, physics, and formalizing neural networks [web:1]. ### GitHub Trending Top 5 Repositories 1. **ruvnet / RuView** (Rust): π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video [web:2]. 2. **K-Dense-AI / claude-scientific-skills** (Python): A set of ready to use Agent Skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance and writing [web:2]. 3. **moeru-ai / airi** (TypeScript): 💖🧸 Self hosted, you-owned Grok Companion, a container of souls of waifu, cyber livings to bring them into our worlds, wishing to achieve Neuro-sama's altitude. Capable of realtime voice chat, Minecraft, Factorio playing. Web / macOS / Windows supported [web:2]. 4. **CodebuffAI / codebuff** (TypeScript): "Generate code from the terminal!" [web:2]. 5. **agentscope-ai / agentscope** (Python): "Build and run agents you can see, understand and trust" [web:2]. ### Inspired Idea: AI-Powered Code Snippet Generator with Contextual Refinement A desktop application that integrates with a developer's IDE, allowing them to highlight existing code and prompt an AI to generate new code snippets based on the highlighted context and user input. The tool would offer contextual refinements such as suggestions for API integration, testing frameworks, and dependency management. This idea is inspired by the themes of AI/LLMs and software engineering from Hacker News, and the GitHub trending repositories focusing on AI agents and code generation. ### Phased 1-Week Execution Plan (Solo Developer) #### Week 1: Minimal Viable Product (MVP) * **Day 1: Setup & Core AI Integration (Time: 8 hours, Budget: $10-20 API)** * **Task:** Project initialization (Python/TypeScript), basic UI framework (e.g., Electron for desktop), integrate with a chosen LLM API (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic). * **Output:** A runnable application that can send a hardcoded prompt to the AI and display the response. * **Day 2: Basic Text Editor Integration & Context Capture (Time: 8 hours, Budget: $0)** * **Task:** Implement functionality to read selected text from a simple text input field within the app. Pass this selected text as part of the AI prompt. * **Output:** User can input code, select a portion, and the AI receives it with a request. * **Day 3: Prompt Engineering for Snippet Generation (Time: 8 hours, Budget: $10-20 API)** * **Task:** Refine AI prompts to effectively generate relevant code snippets based on provided context and a simple user request (e.g., "add a function to calculate factorial"). * **Output:** AI generates basic code snippets that are generally correct. * **Day 4: Output Display & Copy Functionality (Time: 8 hours, Budget: $0)** * **Task:** Display the generated code in a user-friendly format. Implement a "copy to clipboard" button for the generated snippet. * **Output:** Generated code is easily accessible and usable. * **Day 5: Basic Refinement & Iteration (Time: 8 hours, Budget: $5-10 API)** * **Task:** Allow users to provide simple follow-up prompts to refine the generated snippet (e.g., "make it asynchronous"). Gather feedback on initial snippets. * **Output:** AI can iteratively improve snippets based on simple feedback. ### Risks and Mitigations * **Risk: High API Costs:** * **Mitigation:** Start with smaller, cheaper models; implement rate limiting; consider local open-source models for basic tasks; allow users to bring their own API keys. * **Risk: Inaccurate/Irrelevant Code Generation:** * **Mitigation:** Extensive prompt engineering; allow user feedback and refinement loops; integrate static analysis tools to check generated code. * **Risk: Integration Complexity with IDEs:** * **Mitigation:** For MVP, start with a simple text area in the standalone app. For future versions, develop specific IDE extensions (e.g., VS Code extension) as a separate phase. * **Risk: Data Privacy Concerns:** * **Mitigation:** Clearly communicate what data is sent to the AI; allow local model usage; ensure no sensitive project information is transmitted without explicit user consent. * **Risk: Feature Creep:** * **Mitigation:** Stick strictly to the MVP scope for one week. Prioritize core "generate and refine" functionality. ### Compact Budget/Time Table - Category - Time (Hours) - Estimated Cost (USD) - :------- - :----------- - :------------------- - **Development** - 40 (5 days x 8 hours) - $0 (solo developer) - **LLM API Usage** - N/A - $25-50 - **Tools/Software** - N/A - $0 (open-source/free tier) - **Total** - **40** - **$25-50** - <answer> I've analyzed the Hacker News front page [web:1] and GitHub trending repositories [web:2] to formulate a practical proposal. ### Hacker News Top 5 Themes [web:1] 1. **AI/LLMs/Software Engineering:** Discussions around AI, large language models (LLMs), AI agents, code generation, and software verification. 2. **Hardware/OS Advancements:** Focus on new processors, mobile OS developments, and hardware repairability. 3. **Privacy/Security Concerns:** Topics include end-to-end encryption debates and identity/age verification challenges. 4. **Graphics/Game Development:** Resources for graphics programming and innovative game engines/languages. 5. **Scientific/Research Computing:** Articles on complex computational concepts, physics, and formalizing neural networks. ### GitHub Trending Top 5 Repositories [web:2] 1. **ruvnet / RuView** (Rust): WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection. 2. **K-Dense-AI / claude-scientific-skills** (Python): A set of ready to use Agent Skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance and writing. 3. **moeru-ai / airi** (TypeScript): Self hosted, you-owned Grok Companion... Capable of realtime voice chat, Minecraft, Factorio playing. 4. **CodebuffAI / codebuff** (TypeScript): "Generate code from the terminal!" 5. **agentscope-ai / agentscope** (Python): "Build and run agents you can see, understand and trust." ### Inspired Idea: AI-Powered Code Snippet Generator with Contextual Refinement A desktop application that integrates with a developer's IDE. It allows highlighting existing code and prompting an AI to generate new code snippets based on the highlighted context and user input. The tool would offer contextual refinements such as suggestions for API integration, testing frameworks, and dependency management. ### Phased 1-Week Execution Plan (Solo Developer) #### Week 1: Minimal Viable Product (MVP) * **Day 1: Setup & Core AI Integration** (8 hours, Budget: $10-20 API) * Project initialization (Python/TypeScript), basic UI framework (e.g., Electron), integrate with an LLM API. * **Output:** Runnable app sending hardcoded prompts to AI and displaying responses. * **Day 2: Basic Text Editor Integration & Context Capture** (8 hours, Budget: $0) * Implement functionality to read selected text from a simple text input field. Pass this selected text as part of the AI prompt. * **Output:** User can input code, select a portion, and AI receives it. * **Day 3: Prompt Engineering for Snippet Generation** (8 hours, Budget: $10-20 API) * Refine AI prompts to effectively generate relevant code snippets based on provided context and a simple user request. * **Output:** AI generates basic, generally correct code snippets. * **Day 4: Output Display & Copy Functionality** (8 hours, Budget: $0) * Display generated code in a user-friendly format with a "copy to clipboard" button. * **Output:** Generated code is easily accessible and usable. * **Day 5: Basic Refinement & Iteration** (8 hours, Budget: $5-10 API) * Allow users to provide simple follow-up prompts to refine snippets (e.g., "make it asynchronous"). * **Output:** AI can iteratively improve snippets based on simple feedback. ### Risks and Mitigations * **High API Costs:** Start with smaller models, implement rate limiting, consider local open-source models, allow user-provided API keys. * **Inaccurate/Irrelevant Code Generation:** Extensive prompt engineering, user feedback loops, static analysis tool integration. * **Integration Complexity with IDEs:** MVP uses a simple text area; future versions to develop specific IDE extensions. * **Data Privacy Concerns:** Clearly communicate data usage, offer local model options, ensure sensitive data is not transmitted without consent. * **Feature Creep:** Strict adherence to MVP scope for the first week, prioritizing core generation and refinement. ### Compact Budget/Time Table - Category - Time (Hours) - Estimated Cost (USD) - :-------------- - :----------- - :------------------- - **Development** - 40 - $0 - **LLM API Usage** - N/A - $25-50 - **Tools/Software** - N/A - $0 - **Total** - **40** - **$25-50** - </answer></answer>
